compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
ROOT-CAUSE UPDATE (post-deploy diagnosis). The first dc33 fix (change #643, landed) provisions the tickets worktree + parses the rebar-ticket trailer, but the DEPLOYED store came up BROKEN: the boot 'git clone --single-branch --branch tickets' failed silently (masked by the entrypoint '|| echo clone deferred'), leaving an EMPTY repo (git rev-list --count HEAD = 0, HEAD broken). run_ensures then created .env-id on the empty repo, so it LOOKED converged but held zero tickets -> rebar.comment could not resolve any ticket ('not found'). A broken .git also defeats the '[ ! -d .git ]' re-clone guard, so it never self-heals. Separately, a clone-once store goes STALE: the auto-lander (unlike the review-bot, which only CREATES its own artifacts) must resolve tickets OTHER agents created after boot. Manual 'git fetch origin tickets' from the container SUCCEEDS (creds/network fine; dc33 events present on origin/tickets), confirming it's a boot-time clone failure, not an auth issue.

FIX (follow-up change, branch fix-dc33-freshen): close_ticket_via_rebar now FRESHENS the store before annotating - fetch origin tickets + hard reset + run_ensures (re-clones if not a repo) - which self-heals the broken/empty clone AND keeps it current, in one place. Paging policy: unset REBAR_TRACKER_DIR pages (real misconfig, per the reporter's 'missing store is a real error'); a transient refresh failure is a non-paging skip (self-heals next landing); a rebar.comment failure on a FRESH store still pages. Entrypoint also re-fetches a 0-commit clone (defense in depth). Note: the auto-lander CORE (landing) is unaffected and proven (#642, #618 landed cleanly); this is the courtesy-annotation path only.

comments.author: Unknown
comments.body: dc33 store-layout probe #2: comment after a FORCED proper checkout -B tickets origin/tickets (HEAD now a real branch ref).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: ROOT-CAUSE UPDATE #2 + CONFIRMED FIX (post dc33-v2 deploy). dc33 v2's freshen (git fetch origin tickets; git reset --hard FETCH_HEAD; run_ensures) resolves the ticket now, but the rebar.comment COMMIT fails: 'cannot lock ref HEAD: reference already exists'. DIAGNOSIS (confirmed on the live store): after reset --hard FETCH_HEAD the store is left with .git/HEAD -> refs/heads/.invalid and an EMPTY refs/heads/ (no refs/heads/tickets), because FETCH_HEAD reset never establishes a local branch (unlike the review-bot's 'clone --single-branch --branch tickets', which creates refs/heads/tickets + HEAD->it). rebar's commit machinery then cannot lock HEAD. CONFIRMED FIX: forcing 'git checkout -f -B tickets origin/tickets' (proper branch ref; -f to clear run_ensures' staged .gitattributes/.gitignore) makes rebar.comment SUCCEED (verified: COMMENT_OK on the live container). So the freshen (_freshen_ticket_store in infra/autolander/loop.py) + the Dockerfile entrypoint must end the store ON refs/heads/tickets via 'git checkout -f -B tickets origin/tickets' (or reset onto the branch after checking it out), NOT a detached 'reset --hard FETCH_HEAD'. This is the 4th and (expected) final layer of the annotation-store fix. dc33 remains OPEN pending this freshen fix + redeploy + a clean bot-landing annotation. NOTE: bot is PAUSED (emergency-stop) so no landing annotates meanwhile.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: OBSOLETED by the retire-auto-lander migration (epic d4de / retire-code story d0e5): the owner decided (2026-07-13) to drop the bot entirely and land via Gerrit Rebase-If-Necessary + agent Submit. The code carrying this bug (infra/autolander/) is being DELETED, which is this bug's resolution. Close this as obsolete when d0e5 lands; no separate fix will be written.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

The deployed serial auto-lander successfully **landed change #642** (an agent set `Autosubmit+1` and walked away — real dogfood), but the post-merge step that annotates the associated rebar ticket FAILED and emitted a **paging** `AUTOLANDER_ERROR`, tripping the `rebar-autolander-errors` CloudWatch alarm. Two independent real defects:

### Defect 1 — wrong ticket id (code)
`close_ticket_via_rebar(change_id, ticket_id=None, ...)` in `infra/autolander/loop.py` does `tid = ticket_id or change_id`. The submit path (`ancestor_atomic_submit`) does NOT pass `ticket_id`, so it falls back to the **Gerrit Change-Id** (e.g. `Id3915af2d16426d8ec16118622a0b43c88b55377`), which is never a rebar ticket → `rebar comment failed: ticket 'Id39...' not found`. It must parse the `rebar-ticket:` trailer from the landed commit message (available via `get_change(..., ['CURRENT_COMMIT'])`) and use THAT.

### Defect 2 — no ticket store in the container (deployment)
Even with the right id, the annotation fails: `cannot list '/app/.tickets-tracker': No such file or directory`. The autolander container was never provisioned with a rebar tickets worktree. The **review-bot already solves this** (docker-compose.yml: `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=/var/gerrit/site/reviewbot-tickets` + a persistent `gerrit_reviewbot_tickets` volume + `reviewbot-ensure-tickets.sh` + a tickets PAT). The autolander service has NONE of this. S5 deploy overlooked it.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Set `Autosubmit+1` on a submittable change; the bot lands it.
2. Observe `AUTOLANDER_ERROR ticket annotate failed for <Gerrit-Change-Id>: ... ticket not found` and `cannot list '/app/.tickets-tracker'` in the container logs; the `rebar-autolander-errors` alarm fires.

Expected: the bot annotates the correct rebar ticket that the change landed (write + push), and a genuinely-failed annotation does not masquerade as a landing failure.
Actual: paging error after a SUCCESSFUL landing; annotation never happens.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `close_ticket_via_rebar` uses the `rebar-ticket:` trailer parsed from the landed commit, not the Gerrit Change-Id; caller in `ancestor_atomic_submit` passes it.
- [ ] The autolander container is provisioned with a persistent, writable rebar tickets worktree (mirroring the review-bot pattern: `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` + a persistent volume + an ensure-registry bootstrap + a git push credential), so `rebar.comment` on landing succeeds and pushes to origin/tickets.
- [ ] A unit test covers the trailer-parsing/id-selection (Gerrit Change-Id must NOT be used as the ticket id).
- [ ] [operator-attested] After deploy, a bot landing records a landing comment on the correct rebar ticket and the `rebar-autolander-errors` alarm returns to OK.
compiled_state.title: Auto-lander cannot annotate rebar tickets on landing (wrong id + no ticket store in container)
